calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jun-09-2021 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar May-24-2021 Continued To Jun-09-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar May-10-2021 Continued To May-24-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Leon Ainer, via ZOOM, counsel for Maria Leon, Administrator.

The court advises counsel at the outset of its concern with the lack of information shown on the death certificate provided to the court regarding the death of Mark Leon, specifically, the cause of death shown as pending. Counsel confirms that the cause of Mr. Leon's death remains unknown. After additional discussion, the court orders counsel to obtain a recent certified copy of the death certificate, regardless of whether the cause of death has been updated, and file a declaration with the updated, certified copy of the death certificate attached as an exhibit. The court also advises counsel that if the declaration is filed and courtesy copies delivered in a timely manner, the court will consider granting the petition prior to the hearing date.

The First and Final Report of Administrator is continued to May 24, 2021 at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Mar-29-2021 Continued To May-10-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Mar-15-2021 Continued To Mar-29-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Dec-16-2020 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Oct-20-2020 Continued To Dec-16-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances; Leon Ainer appeared by zoom for Maria Leon (Administrator).

The Court stated that it is looking for a petition for final distribution, or a petition for approval of a report on status of administration. Mr. Ainer stated that he submitted a declaration, and that they are at a point close to being able to close out the estate, but there are a number of possible issues including penalties assessed by the City against the property. Mr. Ainer also stated that the home is now vacant, that the Son who lived in the home is deceased, and that the Son may have removed the notices and abatement orders off of the property. In addition, Mr. Ainer stated that they have asked the City for all of the correspondence regarding the abatement orders/complaints, that there will be an upcoming hearing with the City, that he does not know the hearing date, and requested 90-days to determine if there are any claims and to seek permission to encumber the real property to pay the penalties. Furthermore, Mr. Ainer stated that the Son had a life estate in the property, that the Daughter has a remainder interest and that the home is the only asset in the estate. The Court informed Mr. Ainer that he needs to file a petition one way, or the other, that it does not take a declaration in place of the required petitions, that it is going to give him two months and that he either needs to file a petition for final distribution, or a petition for approval of a report on status of administration by then. Status on close of estate is continued to December 16, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Ainer is to have a petition on file by December 9, 2020.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: G. Ideta. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Sep-10-2018 Continued To Oct-01-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Leon Ainer for Maria Leon.

The Court noted that letters have not been issued. Mr. Ainer stated that his Client is petitioning to modify the order from full authority to limited authority with a minimum bond of $20,000.00, that the bonding company has required some extraordinary requirements from his Client, that she was denied, that the Bond Agent is willing to work with them under limited authority and that his Client will file her petition this Friday. Status on close of estate is continued to October 1, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.
